items.title: Introducing Figma Motion: Your Canvas Now Has a Timeline
items.excerpt: Figma Motion brings animation and timing natively onto the design canvas with keyframe timelines, easing controls, and MP4 export — removing the need to hand off motion work to a separate animation tool.

items.title: Figma's Design Agent: Custom Tools, Context, and Skills
items.excerpt: Figma's upgraded AI design agent now supports Skills (packaged team conventions), MCP Connectors to GitHub, Slack and Notion, and shared prompt history — letting agents act on live context from the tools designers already use.

items.title: Rethinking Figma in an AI World
items.excerpt: At Config 2026, Figma responded to AI pressure by expanding its canvas into code layers, motion, and shaders — the real test is no longer competing with other design tools but proving that a canvas-first workflow can stay relevant in a code-first, agentic era.

items.title: AI didn't replace designers — it promoted them
items.excerpt: AI is rewriting the product designer's role from spec-maker to system architect — those who thrive are the ones orchestrating AI rather than competing with it, becoming 'design directors of one' for entire product surfaces.

items.title: Adobe to acquire Topaz Labs
items.excerpt: Adobe announced a definitive agreement to acquire Topaz Labs — maker of Gigapixel AI, Topaz Photo, and Topaz Video — bringing its AI upscaling and NeuroStream on-device inference technology into Firefly and Creative Cloud.

items.title: KeyShot releases KeyShot 2026.2
items.excerpt: KeyShot 2026.2 adds Apple Silicon GPU rendering support (beta), integrates the new cloud-based KeyShot Cloud rendering service, and introduces Node Groups for the material graph — significantly expanding render options for Mac-based design studios.

items.title: Adobe Acquires Topaz Labs, AI Company Offering Photo & Video Editing
items.excerpt: Adobe is acquiring Topaz Labs and its AI-powered Astra video upscaling and Wonder image enhancement tools, with Neurostream technology enabling large AI models to run locally on consumer devices — a major shift for on-device creative AI workflows.

items.title: Gstarsoft updates design portfolio across CAD and BIM
items.excerpt: Gstarsoft launched an integrated CAD+BIM+AI platform combining 2D/3D CAD, BIM, CAM, cloud collaboration, and industry-specific AI models in one connected environment — addressing design and engineering workflows from drawing conversion to AI-assisted visualization.
